name: Lock-Free Programming
def: Concurrent algorithms guaranteeing system-wide progress without mutual-exclusion locks.
problem: Avoid lock-induced blocking, priority inversion and deadlock under contention or in real-time code.
mechanism: Atomic primitives (CAS, fetch-add) and careful memory ordering coordinate threads; reclamation schemes (hazard pointers, epochs) handle freeing.
benefits: Often better tail latency under contention
limits: May be slower than locks under low contention
whenNot: When a mutex is fast enough (usually it is)
myths: "Lock-free = wait-free" — lock-free guarantees some thread progresses; wait-free guarantees all do
